A silicon surface barrier detector with a
sensitive area of 25 mm2 and a depletion depth of
60 mu-m was used. Its detecting surface was placed
13.3 cm from the centre of the target. A 6.3 mu-m mylar
foil placed over the detector prevented it from
Thin self-supporting  boron foil enriched with 11B
and located at the centre  of the chamber was used.
The foils were prepared by  evaporation from a
tantalum boat. Their thicknesses  were determined
from their masses to be approximately  20 mu-g/cm2.
They were mounted on copper frames with  0.6 cm holes
from about 20% for 3He energies near 1.0 MeV to about
The absolute error in the cross section is about a
factor of two. This large error comes from uncertainty
in the thickness of the foil. Errors in the absolute
current measurement and data reduction were not
important compared to the uncertainty in thickness.
The absolute error in the cross section is large (about
a factor of two) because of the uncertainty in target
